steps.title: Estimate the slope from the data range
steps.content:
The scatterplot spans from roughly $(1, 2)$ at the low end to $(5, 15)$ at the high end.

Approximate slope:
$$\text{slope} \approx \frac{15 - 2}{5 - 1} = \frac{13}{4} \approx 3.3$$
The line of best fit needs a slope near 3.3, not 0.82.

steps.title: Eliminate low-slope choices A and B
steps.content: Choices A and B both have slope $0.82$. Over the 4-unit horizontal span, that line would only rise $0.82 \times 4 \approx 3.3$ units — far too shallow for the ~13-unit vertical rise in the data. Eliminate A and B.

steps.title: Choose between C and D using the data center
steps.content:
The data cluster is centered near $(x, y) \approx (3, 11)$. Test $x = 3$:
  • Choice C — $y = 3.30x + 0.82$: $y = 9.90 + 0.82 = 10.72$. Very close to 11. ✓
  • Choice D — $y = 3.30x - 3.30$: $y = 9.90 - 3.30 = 6.60$. Far below the cluster. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.

explanation:
• Choice A — '$y = 0.82x + 3.30$': Slope 0.82 is far too shallow for data that rises ~13 units over 4 units of $x$. ✗
  • Choice B — '$y = 0.82x - 0.82$': Same incorrectly shallow slope; negative intercept makes predictions even lower. ✗
  • Choice C — '$y = 3.30x + 0.82$': Slope 3.3 matches the estimated rise/run; at the data center $(3, 11)$ the line predicts $\approx 10.72$. ✓
  • Choice D — '$y = 3.30x - 3.30$': Slope is correct, but the intercept $-3.30$ pulls the line too low — predicts $6.60$ at $x = 3$, well below the data center. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
